channel.title: Sqwod Daily
channel.description: The business of fitness, every weekday.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 07 July 2026
item.description: Before you renew that Meta ad spend, check what's already growing for free in your group chats.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 07 July 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Before you renew that Meta ad spend, check what's already growing for free in your group chats.
— Sqwod
Move of the Day
Watch on Instagram →
The rundown
01
DSSV legt Eckdaten der Fitnesswirtschaft DACH 2026 vor
Der Branchenverband DSSV hat die aktuellen Kennzahlen für den Fitnessmarkt in Deutschland, Österreich und der Schweiz veröffentlicht — für Studio-Betreiber und Trainer die Zahlen, an denen man sich in diesem Jahr messen lassen muss.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE bringt Top-Expertise live ins Studio-Webinar
body LIFE lädt Studio-Betreiber zu einem exklusiven Live-Webinar mit Top-Expertise ein. Wer sein Studio weiterbringen will, sollte diesen Termin nicht verpassen — echtes Fachwissen, direkt anwendbar.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Loop Air brings GPS and ECG to budget wearables
A new Whoop alternative called Loop Air is offering GPS and ECG tracking at a lower price point — worth a look if your clients want data-rich recovery tools without the premium subscription sting.
Source: Notebookcheck →
04
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for movement as a therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches that the science backs what you've been selling all along.
Source: Trainingsworld →
05
Community Is the New Marketing Budget
Fitness and wellness brands are ditching traditional ads and leaning on their communities as a full-blown growth engine — which means the tribe you've been building in your studio might be your most underrated business asset.
Source: Athletech News →
06
Garmin Forerunner 70 Hits India With a Price Tag and Specs
Garmin has launched the Forerunner 70 in India, and TelecomTalk has the full breakdown on what it costs and what it does.
Source: TelecomTalk →
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Run a 'bring a friend free for a week' window tied to an existing challenge or milestone — referrals spike when there's a natural social moment to attach them to, not just an open invitation.
✓ Track: Start measuring Net Promoter Score monthly with a single post-class text: 'How likely are you to recommend us to a friend, 0–10?' Trends in that number predict churn and word-of-mouth growth before you see either on your P&L.
✓ Try: Create one member-only touchpoint that has nothing to do with training — a monthly coffee, a Spotify playlist they help curate, a members-only Q&A — something that builds identity around belonging, not just workouts.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Garmin's Forerunner 70 just dropped in India — one of the world's fastest-growing fitness markets — which means the era of 'my client doesn't track data' is quietly ending on multiple continents simultaneously.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 06 July 2026
item.description: Plunges, pads, and boutique content drops — today's fitness business is being built in the spaces between the reps.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 06 July 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Plunges, pads, and boutique content drops — today's fitness business is being built in the spaces between the reps.
— Sqwod
Money movement
▲  Raise
Heatronics
 Rs 1.8 crore (₹18M)
seed round led by Inflection Point Ventures to scale wellness hardware
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Officially a Wellness Industry Talking Point
The Global Wellness Institute is putting contrast hydrotherapy on the map — meaning your clients are about to start asking about cold plunges and hot soaks whether you're ready or not. Now's a good time to have an answer.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Rhone Is Betting Big on IRL — Stores, Events, the Works
The activewear brand's CEO says in-person shopping and live experiences are a core growth play, not an afterthought. For studio owners and operators, that's a timely reminder that the physical floor still sells.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Fitness On Demand Just Got a Boutique Studio Upgrade
Fitness On Demand is teaming up with JETSWEAT to bring more boutique studio content to its platform. For operators looking to keep members sweating without building a content library from scratch, this one's worth a look.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
04
Rs 1.8 Cr Backs Heatronics to Heat Up the Wellness Device Space
Inflection Point Ventures led the seed round for wellness hardware startup Heatronics — a signal that investors are warming up to recovery and device-led fitness beyond just apps. If you're a studio operator eyeing hardware partnerships, the funding pipeline just got a little more interesting.
Source: Indian Startup Times →
05
Heatronics Pulls In Rs 1.8 Crore for Wellness Devices
The Indian wellness tech firm just closed a Rs 1.8 crore raise to scale its hardware business — a reminder that the market for recovery and performance devices has real investment heat behind it, not just consumer hype.
Source: Siliconindia →
06
Movement is Medicine: Wolfgang Unsöld Makes the Case
Trainingsworld's latest argues that movement isn't just exercise — it's therapy. For coaches looking to deepen client buy-in, the science-backed framing is a ready-made conversation starter.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Stat
Rs 1.8 crore
seed funding raised by Heatronics
It's a small number globally, but it signals that investors in emerging markets are writing real checks for recovery hardware — the category is attracting capital, not just consumer curiosity.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your front-desk cancel and pause reasons for 90 days — most operators discover that 'life got busy' is code for 'I stopped feeling results,' which is exactly the gap a recovery offering closes.
✓ Steal: Build a 3-email welcome sequence that explicitly teaches new members how to use your space between classes — stretching cues, recovery tips, hydration prompts. Retention starts at orientation, not at month three.
✓ Try: Run a single 'themed open house' event in your studio — invite members to bring one guest and attach it to a specific skill, modality, or topic night. In-person community moments drive referrals more reliably than discount codes.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Rhone is opening more stores because apparently 'just buy it online' wasn't the whole answer — a lesson the fitness industry learned the hard way and then forgot, and is now learning again.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 03 July 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 03 July 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
▲  Raise
Heatronics
 Rs 1.8 crore (₹18M)
seed round led by Inflection Point Ventures to scale heat-based wellness devices
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why
The Global Wellness Institute is putting contrast hydrotherapy on the radar, and for coaches and studio operators, that means one thing: your clients are about to start asking questions. Get ahead of it.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Rhone Is Betting Big on Brick-and-Mortar and IRL Events
The activewear brand's CEO says they're doubling down on in-person shopping and events — a reminder that for fitness-adjacent brands, the gym floor (and the store floor) still beats the scroll.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Fitness On Demand Just Got a Boutique Upgrade
Fitness On Demand has partnered with JETSWEAT to bring more boutique studio content to its platform — meaning operators can now offer members that curated, high-energy class feel without needing to actually build out the studio.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
04
Rs 1.8 Crore Bet: Heatronics Is Heating Up the Wellness Device Space
Indian startup Heatronics just closed a Rs 1.8 crore raise to scale its wellness device business. If you're watching the hardware side of recovery and performance tech, this one's worth keeping on your radar.
Source: Siliconindia →
05
Heatronics Heats Up With Rs 1.8 Cr Seed Round
Wellness device startup Heatronics has raised Rs 1.8 crore in seed funding led by Inflection Point Ventures. The fresh capital will fuel expansion of its wellness device business.
Source: Indian Startup Times →
06
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for exercise as a genuine therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches and studio owners that the science behind what you do is your strongest sales pitch.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your revenue-per-square-foot monthly — not just total revenue. It forces honest questions about which physical spaces are earning their keep and which are just costing you lease.
✓ Steal: Build a one-page 'science card' for your top three signature modalities — one study, one plain-English sentence, one client outcome. Post it at the relevant station. Turns your methodology into credibility without a single paid ad.
✓ Try: Run a 60-minute 'founder office hours' session inside your studio once a month — open to members, no agenda. The intel you get on what they actually want is worth more than most member surveys.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Wolfgang Unsöld is out here using peer-reviewed science as a sales pitch, which is either the future of fitness marketing or the world's longest SOAP note.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 02 July 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 02 July 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
Precision is the new premium in fitness
Three things landed this week that look unrelated — a longevity window for strength, a women's-only lifting studio, and a hydrotherapy revival — but they're all saying the same thing: vague wellness is losing, specificity is winning. Clients don't want 'move more'; they want a protocol, a number, a room designed exactly for them. The operators building around precise outcomes are the ones capturing boutique dollars right now.
This is your competitive moat. When the Global Wellness Institute publishes a tidy strength window and a studio concept launches around a single demographic's lifting identity, the market is signaling that operators who can answer 'why this, how much, for whom' will out-retain and out-acquire everyone still selling general fitness vibes. Precision isn't a niche play anymore — it's the product.
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why
The Global Wellness Institute is shining a spotlight on contrast hydrotherapy as a rising recovery modality. If you're not already talking hot-cold protocols with your clients, now's a good time to warm up to the idea.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Founders Row launches LiftHer, a women's strength studio
Founders Row is entering the women's strength space with its new LiftHer studio concept — a signal that dedicated female lifting environments are moving from niche to next big thing for operators watching where boutique dollars are flowing.
Source: Athletech News →
03
The Best Fitness & Wellness Launches This Season, Ranked
From the Oura Ring 5 to an AI-powered scented sleep gadget, Athletech News rounded up this season's most notable fitness and wellness drops — worth a scan if you're advising clients on gear or scouting what's actually cutting through the noise.
Source: Athletech News →
04
The Weekly Workout Sweet Spot for a Longer Life? 90–119 Mins
A new study covered by the Global Wellness Institute found that 90–119 minutes of strength training per week may be the magic window for longevity. Not too little, not too much — just right, says the science.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
Stat
90–119 min/week
Weekly strength training for maximum longevity impact
This is the number to build programming blocks around — not because it's a round target, but because it's specific enough to hand a client and watch their commitment sharpen. Ranges outperform vague minimums for adherence.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Start logging client retention by format type (strength vs. cardio vs. recovery) — if you don't know which modality retains longest at your studio, you're pricing and scheduling on instinct, not signal.
✓ Steal: Borrow the 'designed for one demographic' brief from specialty studio concepts: pick your highest-LTV client segment and write a one-page studio-within-a-studio concept for them — even if you never build it, the exercise sharpens your positioning.
✓ Try: Run a 10-minute contrast protocol (hot finish followed by a cold rinse) as an optional add-on after one weekly class, track NPS separately for those who try it vs. those who don't — cheap way to validate recovery demand before investing in hardware.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Somewhere out there, a gadget that pumps scented air at you while you sleep is being called a wellness launch of the season. The bar for 'cutting through the noise' has never been more fragrant.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 01 July 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 01 July 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why
The Global Wellness Institute is shining a spotlight on contrast hydrotherapy as a rising recovery modality. If you're programming recovery for clients, this is the trend worth getting ahead of before your competitors do.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Pure Barre Pulls Big Numbers With Studio-Wide Livestream
Pure Barre's studio-wide livestream drew serious turnout — proof that when you give members a reason to show up (even virtually), they will. For operators eyeing community-at-scale plays, this one's worth a look.
Source: Athletech News →
03
CrossFit and WurQ Are Building a Functional Fitness Wearable
CrossFit has partnered with WurQ to develop a wearable built specifically for functional fitness movements — which means the data your clients actually care about might finally have a device to match.
Source: Athletech News →
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Borrow Pure Barre's studio-wide livestream playbook to create a recurring virtual event that gives members a reason to show up together — even from home.
✓ Try: Add contrast hydrotherapy (or a partnership with a local facility that offers it) to your recovery programming before competitors in your market do.
✓ Read: Track the CrossFit x WurQ wearable development — functional fitness-specific data will change how coaches program and how clients perceive progress.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Pure Barre figured out that 'virtual' and 'packed house energy' aren't mutually exclusive — meanwhile the rest of the industry is still arguing about whether hybrid is real.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 30 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 30 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The body keeps the score — and so does your studio
Three stories today share a spine most operators will miss: the body isn't a footnote to your business strategy, it's the mechanism. GLP-1 clients need exercise to get the full benefit. Leaders on four hours of sleep are neurologically compromised decision-makers. Wolfgang Unsöld is arguing movement is treatment, not just training. The thread? Biology is the product, whether you've positioned it that way or not.
For studio founders, that's a reframe worth money. You're not selling classes — you're the clinical complement to the pharmaceuticals your clients are already paying for, the sleep debt their cortisol is already accruing, and the mental health system that's chronically underfunded. That's not a marketing line. That's a defensible category. The operators who internalize it first will write the new playbook that Athletech News says everyone else urgently needs.
The rundown
01
Your Brain on a Bad Day: The Biology Behind Tough Calls
The Global Wellness Institute unpacks how pressure physically reshapes the way leaders make decisions — which means if you're running a studio on four hours of sleep and a cold brew, your biology is quietly voting against you.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Boutique Studios: Your Old Marketing Playbook Is Expiring Fast
Athletech News flags that marketing tactics for boutique fitness studios are shifting at serious speed. If your strategy still looks like 2022, it might be time to tear up the script and write a new one.
Source: Athletech News →
03
GLP-1 drugs work better with exercise, study confirms
A new multinational study finds that exercise improves the long-term benefits of GLP-1 medications — which means your clients on Ozempic aren't off the hook, they're actually your best argument for showing up consistently.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
04
Garmin Forerunner 170 Music lands a Women's Running verdict
Women's Running just dropped its review of the Garmin Forerunner 170 Music — if you're recommending GPS watches to clients who want beats with their splits, this one's worth a read before your next gear conversation.
Source: Women's Running →
05
Oura Ring 5 Is a 'Generational Leap' for Smart Rings
The Guardian calls the Oura Ring 5 a stunning generational upgrade — for coaches and studio owners recommending wearables to clients, it just got a lot easier to point to a clear leader in the category.
Source: The Guardian →
06
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld has the receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case that movement isn't just training — it's treatment. For coaches and studio operators, that's a positioning goldmine: your floor isn't a gym, it's a clinic with better playlists.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Sqwod recs
✓ Read: Athletech News on boutique fitness marketing shifts — treat it as a checklist and cross off every tactic you're still running from 2022.
✓ Steal: Unsöld's 'movement is therapy' framing for your next intake form or welcome email — reposition your studio floor before a competitor does.
✓ Try: Before your next big operational decision, check: how much sleep did you get? The GWI research gives you scientific cover to actually protect your recovery.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Garmin and Oura dropped new hardware the same week — apparently wearables didn't get the memo that the economy is supposed to be cooling.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 29 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 29 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
SotoMethod Takes Its NYC Sweat Formula to Dallas
The boutique studio is making its first move outside New York City, planting a flag in Dallas as the opening act of a national expansion. For studio operators watching the boutique space, it's a reminder that the brands scaling right now aren't waiting for perfect conditions — they're just picking a city and going.
Source: Athletech News →
02
Your Brain on Pressure: The Biology of Leading Under Stress
The Global Wellness Institute is diving into the biology of decision-making under pressure — because it turns out your leadership style and your nervous system are in a complicated relationship.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
03
The Global Strength Equipment Market Just Got a Full Breakdown
IndexBox dropped a deep-dive analysis on the world strength training equipment market — size, trends, forecasts, the works. If you're planning your next equipment investment or expanding your floor, this is the kind of data that turns gut feelings into actual strategy.
Source: IndexBox →
04
Garmin Forerunner 170 Music reviewed by Women's Running
Women's Running put Garmin's Forerunner 170 Music through its paces — if you're recommending GPS watches to running clients, this hands-on take is worth a look before your next gear conversation.
Source: Women's Running →
05
Brownlee & Bryant Are Bringing AI to the Fitness Congress
Triathlon legend Alistair Brownlee and performance expert Steffie Bryant are headlining the Health & Fitness Association's European Congress with a session on AI and performance insights — if you're a coach or operator wondering how to actually use AI beyond the hype, this one's worth your calendar.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
06
Wareable just ranked the best fitness trackers of 2026
Wareable dropped their fully reviewed, tested, and compared guide to the top fitness trackers on the market right now. If clients keep asking what to wear on their wrist, you finally have a cheat sheet worth bookmarking.
Source: Wareable →
Sqwod recs
✓ Read: Skim the IndexBox strength equipment market breakdown before your next floor or equipment budget conversation — it turns 'I think free weights are trending' into an actual sentence you can defend.
✓ Watch: Add the Brownlee & Bryant AI session at HFA's European Congress to your calendar — triathlon-level performance thinking applied to AI is a more useful lens than another chatbot demo.
✓ Steal: Before recommending a GPS watch to a running client, send them the Women's Running Garmin Forerunner 170 review — it does the selling for you and positions you as the coach who actually does homework.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Wareable just published the definitive 2026 fitness tracker rankings — meaning your clients will walk in this week armed with a review for a product that technically isn't out yet. Godspeed.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 26 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 26 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
Identity is infrastructure — for bodies, brands, and cities
Every story this week is quietly about the same thing: what happens when something gets to become more fully itself. A PT finds his training identity post-surgery. A Thai island cluster finds its wellness identity through sustainability. A boutique method finds its national identity by leaving New York. SotoMethod, Krabi, and a 21-year-old coach aren't adjacent stories — they're the same story wearing different shoes.
For operators, the pattern is worth stealing: the studios, destinations, and trainers pulling ahead right now aren't adding more offerings. They're sharpening what they already are until the signal is impossible to ignore. Your brand isn't what you do — it's what people feel certain about when they think of you. That clarity is what turns one location into a growth story, one island into a wellness magnet, and one trainer's personal journey into a coaching philosophy that clients actually remember.
The rundown
01
Your Brain on Leadership: Why Pressure Rewires Your Calls
The Global Wellness Institute is digging into the biology behind high-stakes decision-making — and for coaches and studio owners, understanding what stress actually does to your brain mid-crunch could be the difference between a smart pivot and a panic move.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Becoming Wellness Gold
Lokmattimes spotlights how sustainability and authentic Thai hospitality are shaping the wellness scene in Krabi and Koh Lanta — worth a look if you're scouting retreat destinations or want to know where your clients are dragging their yoga mats next.
Source: lokmattimes.com →
03
SotoMethod Takes Its NYC Sweat Formula to Dallas
The boutique studio is planting its first flag outside New York City, kicking off a national expansion push. For operators watching the boutique space, it's a reminder that a strong method plus the right moment can turn a single location into a growth story.
Source: Athletech News →
04
Cannes 2026 Adds 6 New Categories—Here's What's In
Audio & Radio, Health & Wellness, Pharma, Creative B2B, Print & Publishing, and Outdoor are all getting their own spotlight at Cannes Lions 2026 — meaning the work your studio does to market itself just got a much bigger stage to compete on.
Source: Creative Salon →
05
21-Year-Old PT on How Top Surgery Reshaped His Fitness Identity
People.com spotlights a young personal trainer whose top surgery and fitness journey became inseparable parts of finding himself. For coaches, it's a reminder that identity isn't a sidebar to training — sometimes it's the whole program.
Source: People.com →
06
Sport Alliance is rewriting fitness's AI playbook
body LIFE reports that Sport Alliance is actively reshaping how the fitness industry approaches AI — meaning the tools your competitors are experimenting with today could be table stakes by tomorrow.
Source: body LIFE →
Sqwod recs
✓ Read: Global Wellness Institute's breakdown of stress biology and decision-making — if you're making calls about pricing, staffing, or pivots under pressure, knowing what cortisol actually does to risk assessment is table stakes.
✓ Steal: SotoMethod's expansion framing: they led with method, not locations. Before you think about a second site or a franchise conversation, make sure your first location has a replicable, nameable system — not just vibes.
✓ Try: Ask Sport Alliance or any AI tool you're already using one operational question this week you'd normally Google. The gap between 'experimenting' and 'using' closes faster than operators expect — and the gap between early adopters and everyone else is where margin lives.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Cannes Lions added a Health & Wellness category for 2026 — so yes, your next email campaign about foam rolling could theoretically win a trophy next to a Coca-Cola spot. No pressure.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 25 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 25 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The fitness industry is getting a biology upgrade
From the Global Wellness Institute unpacking how cortisol literally rewires your judgment under pressure, to Sport Alliance deploying AI across gym operations, to the Health & Fitness Association planting flags across Asia — the throughline is the same: the gap between operators who understand the underlying systems (biological, technological, geographic) and those who just follow trends is widening fast. Your competitors aren't just buying better equipment. They're building better infrastructure for decisions.
And it's not stopping at the gym floor. Loya's anti-grey haircare line and Thailand's sustainability-forward retreat model are both using the same playbook your top clients already trust: science-meets-experience positioning that commands premium pricing. The wellness industry is quietly colonizing beauty, travel, and leadership coaching — and the studios that notice early get to be the connective tissue their clients didn't know they needed.
The rundown
01
Your Brain on a Bad Day: The Biology Behind Big Calls
The Global Wellness Institute is digging into how pressure physically hijacks your decision-making — which means the way you show up to tough conversations with staff or clients isn't just mindset, it's biology.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Crunch Fitness Has a New Boss: Meet Chequan Lewis
Crunch Fitness has named Chequan Lewis as its new CEO in what the company is calling a major leadership move. For operators and studio founders, it's worth watching — leadership changes at a chain this size tend to signal where the big-box market is headed next.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI
Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively transforming how the fitness sector operates through AI — meaning the tech your competitors are quietly testing today could be the standard your studio is judged against tomorrow.
Source: body LIFE →
04
Swiss Brand Loya Is Coming for Your Grey Hairs
Longevity label Loya has launched an anti-grey haircare line, bringing the wellness-meets-science positioning that sells so well in fitness into the beauty aisle. If your clients are already buying into recovery tech and biohacking, this is the next category they'll be spending on.
Source: BeautyNewsDaily →
05
Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Rewriting the Wellness Retreat Playbook
Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into something coaches and retreat operators can't easily replicate at home. If you're scouting locations for an immersive program, this corner of the Gulf is worth more than a casual Google.
Source: lokmattimes.com →
06
Health & Fitness Association Is Taking Its Show to Asia
The Health & Fitness Association is expanding its presence across Asia through presentations at major industry events. For operators eyeing growth beyond their home markets, consider this your cue to pay attention.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
Sqwod recs
✓ Watch: Track Chequan Lewis's first moves as Crunch CEO — leadership pivots at big-box chains telegraph where franchise incentives, pricing models, and member acquisition strategies are heading for the whole market.
✓ Steal: Borrow Loya's positioning logic: take one recovery or performance service you already offer and reframe it with a longevity or science-backed angle in your next email to clients.
✓ Read: Look up Sport Alliance's AI rollout via Body Life — understanding what's being automated at scale tells you exactly which parts of your operations are becoming table stakes vs. genuine differentiators.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Somewhere in Switzerland, a longevity brand looked at grey hair and thought: that's not aging, that's an unmonetized biohack. Respect.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself

Subscribe free

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 24 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 24 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The infrastructure of legitimacy is being built around you
Look at today's feed as a single frame: lifestyle medicine earns clinical credibility in cancer care, AI rewires gym operations, recovery becomes a headline partnership asset, and a new CEO lands at one of the biggest chains in the game. These aren't isolated updates — they're the same story told six ways. The fitness industry is graduating from "nice to have" to essential infrastructure, and the organizations paying attention are staking their claims right now.
For operators, the window to position early is narrowing fast. The coaches who document clinical relevance, the studios that bake recovery into their core offer, the founders who treat AI as a tool rather than a threat — they're not ahead of a trend, they're already inside a structural shift. The ones who wait for the trade press consensus are going to be optimizing for a market that already moved.
The rundown
01
Lifestyle Medicine Is Now a Cancer Care Power Player
The Global Wellness Institute says lifestyle interventions matter across the entire cancer journey — from prevention all the way through survivorship. For coaches and studio owners, that's a signal: your work isn't just wellness fluff, it's clinically relevant territory worth leaning into.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Crunch Fitness Has a New CEO — Meet Chequan Lewis
Crunch Fitness has tapped Chequan Lewis as its new chief executive in what the chain is calling a major leadership move. For operators watching the big-box space, a fresh face at the top usually signals a strategic reset — worth keeping an eye on where Crunch points next.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Cycling BC Keeps the Recovery Rolling With 360 Athletics
Cycling BC has renewed its partnership with Recovery Room, the sister brand of 360 Athletics — a reminder that for sports orgs and operators alike, the recovery offering is becoming just as important as the ride itself.
Source: Cycling BC →
04
Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Becoming Wellness Operator Gold
Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into a compelling wellness travel package. For retreat operators and studio founders eyeing international programming, this region is worth a serious look before everyone else catches on.
Source: lokmattimes.com →
05
Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI
Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively changing how the fitness sector works through artificial intelligence — meaning the tech wave isn't coming for coaches and operators, it's already in the building.
Source: body LIFE →
06
A NZ supplement brand just pulled creatine — now what?
A New Zealand supplement brand has yanked creatine from shelves, leaving a gap in a lot of client stacks. Worth knowing your alternatives before someone asks you mid-session.
Source: NZ Herald →
Sqwod recs
✓ Watch: Track where Chequan Lewis steers Crunch in his first 90 days — new big-box CEOs telegraph strategic gaps that independent operators can exploit before the pivot is public.
✓ Steal: Cycling BC's partnership framing is worth copying: they renewed with a recovery brand by treating recovery as core to the sport, not an add-on. Reframe your recovery offering the same way in your next member communication.
✓ Read: Pull the Global Wellness Institute's lifestyle medicine and cancer care findings — even one cited data point makes your coach bios and intake materials significantly harder to dismiss by healthcare referral partners.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
A New Zealand supplement brand pulled creatine from shelves, which is a remarkable way to start a conversation with every client who just asked you what creatine even does. Consider it a free education moment, not a crisis.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 23 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 23 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The fitness business rewards the second look
An inconclusive autopsy, a property tax bill that quietly compounded, a wearable nobody wore past week two — the theme isn't bad luck, it's deferred attention. Operators who only check the obvious numbers (revenue, retention) keep getting surprised by the stuff living one layer down: how their square footage is assessed, whether the gear they recommend actually sticks, what slips through the cracks during a packed coaching day.
The flip side is also true. Fitbod's CEO has been watching the same signal since day one — resistance training doesn't fade — and that patience is now being validated by where AI coaching tools are placing their bets. The operators winning right now aren't the ones reacting to trends; they're the ones who looked closer, earlier, and kept looking.
The rundown
01
SC Trainer's Autopsy Raises More Questions Than Answers
The autopsy of a South Carolina fitness trainer has deepened the mystery surrounding their death rather than resolving it. For coaches and studio operators, it's a reminder that when something seems off, the full picture rarely surfaces on the first look.
Source: Yahoo →
02
Waukee Kettlestone Gym Owner Settles Tax Dispute With Dallas County
A Waukee, Iowa gym owner has reached a settlement with Dallas County over a property tax dispute, per the Des Moines Register. If you own your space, it's a reminder that how your property is assessed can quietly eat into your margins — worth a periodic audit of what you're actually being taxed on.
Source: The Des Moines Register →
03
Fitbit Air named most comfortable tracker ever worn
Mashable SEA's verdict is in: the Fitbit Air takes the top spot for wearable comfort — worth knowing if you're recommending trackers to clients who've ditched their last one by week two.
Source: Mashable SEA →
04
This Tiny Wearable AI Pin Takes Notes So You Don't Have To
AndroidGuys went hands-on with the Plaud NotePin S, a wearable AI note-taker built for in-person work — for coaches and studio operators who are too busy actually coaching to scribble things down, this one's worth a look.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
From day one to today, Fitbod's CEO says resistance training has never stopped being the core of the fitness business — and it's the same logic fueling the rise of AI coaching tools like Google's Health Coach. If you're a studio owner wondering where to double down, the data keeps pointing to the weight room.
Source: TechRadar →
Policy watch
Property Tax Assessments: The Silent Margin Killer
A Waukee, Iowa gym owner just settled a property tax dispute with Dallas County — a reminder that local assessors don't always classify fitness spaces correctly, and the appeals process exists for a reason. If you own your building, an incorrect assessment can drain margin year after year before anyone notices.
Sqwod recs
✓ Try: Test the Fitbit Air with one or two chronically non-compliant clients — the ones who ditched their last tracker by week two. Comfort is an underrated compliance variable.
✓ Steal: Borrow Fitbod's single-signal discipline: pick the one service (strength, recovery, small-group) that has never stopped growing in your studio and build your next hire or programming cycle around it.
✓ Try: If you're coaching back-to-back and losing session notes, look up the Plaud NotePin S — a wearable AI that captures in-person conversations so you're not choosing between being present and keeping records.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Fitbit's new Air just won 'most comfortable tracker ever worn' — which is a lovely honor that also implies every previous Fitbit was, at some level, a mild annoyance strapped to your wrist. Progress.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 22 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 22 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
⇄  Acquired
Peloton
 —
acquired connected Pilates brand Skōp, adding the discipline to its fitness portfolio
Connect the dots
The fitness industry is eating its own homework
Peloton buying a connected Pilates brand, Garmin dropping a $349 runner's watch, and Fitbod's CEO reminding everyone that strength training never left — these aren't separate headlines. They're the same story: hardware and software companies are racing to own the full-stack fitness experience before studios do it themselves. The discipline doesn't matter. The data does.
For operators, the pattern is clear: every category you're not capturing digitally is a category someone with a balance sheet is circling. Connected Pilates just got acquired. Connected strength is already commoditized. The question isn't whether your modality will get the Peloton treatment — it's whether you'll still be relevant when it does.
The rundown
01
Peloton just bought connected Pilates brand Skōp
Peloton has acquired Skōp, a connected Pilates innovator, adding the discipline to its expanding fitness portfolio. For studio operators, it's a signal that connected Pilates is officially too big for the big players to ignore.
Source: Yahoo! Finance Canada →
02
She tried to cancel her gym membership. The billing company didn't care.
A member showed up to cancel in person, found no one there, and has been charged ever since — a reminder that unclear cancellation processes aren't just a member frustration, they're a liability waiting to go viral.
Source: Yahoo Finance →
03
$349 Garmin Forerunner 170 Music: A Lot of Watch
Runner's World is calling Garmin's new Forerunner 170 Music a serious value proposition at $349 — if your clients are asking what to strap on their wrists, you've got an answer ready.
Source: Runner's World →
04
Plaud NotePin S Is the AI Recorder You Can Actually Wear
Plaud's NotePin S is a wearable AI recorder designed to capture notes hands-free — which means coaches and studio founders can finally stop fumbling for their phones mid-session and just talk.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
Fitbod's CEO tells TechRadar that resistance training has been the core of health and fitness since day one — and for coaches and operators wondering why AI health chatbots are suddenly everywhere, the answer starts in the weight room.
Source: TechRadar →
Stat
$349
retail price of the Garmin Forerunner 170 Music
When Runner's World calls a watch a 'serious value proposition' at this price point, your clients will find the review before you do — knowing the answer before the question arrives is the whole job.
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Borrow Fitbod's positioning playbook: anchor your programming narrative in strength as the original health behavior, then layer AI or tech on top — not the other way around
✓ Try: Test the Plaud NotePin S for post-session voice notes — capturing coaching insights hands-free means your IP stops living exclusively in your head
✓ Read: Pull up your cancellation policy today and walk through it as if you're a frustrated member at 7pm on a Tuesday — fix whatever breaks
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
A member tried to cancel her gym membership in person, found no one there, and kept getting charged. Somewhere, a billing software salesperson is using this as a case study. The gym is using it as a cautionary tale. The difference is about $12 a month and one very angry post.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe
